headline: Expensive Coffee
subtext: As ratepayers fork out for their daily brew, we explore the reasons behind the rising costs and how local councils contribute to the problem.
body:
Worry not, fellow caffeine addicts! The Remy Digest has uncovered the reason behind the outrageous prices of your daily cuppa - it's all thanks to those meddling council types.

According to a recent report from our crack team of investigators (who we've now nicknamed "The Coffee Commandos"), local councils have been enforcing strict vocabulary rules on coffee shops. This includes the mandatory use of "Councillor" or its more casual acronym, "Cr," instead of the archaic term "Councilman." It gets worse: these word-nazis are also insisting that we call the place where they hold their meetings "Council Chambers" and not "City Hall."

But wait, there's more! These linguistic tyrants have decreed that we refer to our refuse as "Rubbish" or "Garbage," never using the Americanism "Trash." And let's not forget their obsession with rebranding our paved footpaths as "footpaths," rather than succumbing to the temptation of calling them "sidewalks."

So, what does all this linguistic shenanigans have to do with your morning latte? Well, it turns out that these stringent spelling rules mean coffee shops are spending a small fortune on rebranding efforts. And before you start frothing at the mouth, let's not forget that we're in Australia - land of the long 'O', and the ever-persistent "I before E" rule.

But don't take our word for it. We spoke to a local ratepayer (who, incidentally, is also a conspiracy theorist who believes the moon landing was faked). They said: "This whole thing with coffee prices is ridiculous! I mean, how hard can it be to spell 'Organise' or 'Colour'? It's not like we're trying to figure out the meaning of life here. Just pour me another cup, will ya?"

Well, there you have it - the truth about high-priced coffee has been revealed. So next time you're splashing the cash on your morning brew, just remember: it's all in the name of upholding our nation's linguistic integrity.

professor.title: From The Archives
professor.content: The archives are currently closed due to a flea infestation.

mystic.prediction: As The Mystic, I foresee a time of prosperity for Aquarians on the Northern Beaches. A new coffee shop will open near your favorite surfing spot, and parking won't be as much of an issue due to increased real estate investment in the area. Despite the ominous predictions about the B-Line bus and Bin Chickens, your horoscope remains relatively mundane compared to other star signs' predicaments.
